title: Germany's LEAG suspends H2UB Green Hydrogen Plan
content: LEAG, a major German energy firm, announced it is indefinitely delaying its proposed H2UB Boxberg projectâ€”one of Europeâ€™s most ambitious green energy hubsâ€”planned for construction on the site of retired coal-fired power plant units in eastern Germany. The company attributed the decision to political and economic conditions that have failed to progress as anticipated. This setback follows a similar announcement from ArcelorMittal, which recently scrapped plans to transition two of its German steel plants to carbon-neutral operations, also due to high energy costs. Together, the developments reflect growing concerns about the investment climate for large-scale decarbonization projects in Germany, particularly amid rising operational expenses and uncertain policy support.
author: Analysts Account
